# Preprocessing (The Assembly)
-   the how to section
-   instructions to process/generate files
-   how to format data
-   link to video(s)


## Environmental Setup

-   Standard directory structure
-   naming convention

## Raster Processing

-   Spatial processing using WhiteboxTools and R

WhiteboxTools is a collection of geospatial analysis tools used throughout this workflow. The whitebox R package provides an interface to these tools, but the WhiteboxTools executable must also be installed.
the command whitebox::install_whitebox() downloads a precompiled executable (binary) for the user's operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux).

In the process_spatial_inputs.Rmd file
dem <- RHESSysPreprocessing::download_terrain_variables(bbox = bbox_landscape,
                                                        data_source = "FedData",
                                                        variable = "dem",
                                                        label = "sagehen",
                                                        res_ned = "1",
                                                        proj_epsg = utm_epsg,
                                                        res_final = 90,
                                                        res_final_example = NULL,
                                                        output_file = "dem_landscape.tif")

the res_ned option controls the source data resolution (NED product)
-   1 arc-second is ~30 meters (CONUS standard)
-   1/3 arc-second is ~10 meters (available for some areas including much of the western US)
-   1/9 arc-second is ~3 meters (limited high-resolution coverage)

setting res_ned to 1 retrieves a 30 meter DEM, setting res_ned to 13 retrieves a 10 meter DEM
use the command terra::res(dem) to find out the resolution of the dem object


NED Product	Approx. Resolution	Typical Use	Strengths	Limitations
1/9 arc-second	~3 m	Very small watersheds, research on hillslope processes	Extremely detailed topography	Very expensive computationally; often too noisy for basin-scale RHESSys

1/3 arc-second	~10 m	High-resolution watershed studies	Captures fine drainage features, realistic hillslopes	Large datasets; can introduce over-fragmented flow paths

1 arc-second	~30 m	Standard RHESSys choice (recommended default)	Good balance of realism + computational efficiency	Misses fine terrain detail

3 arc-second (older DEM products)	~90 m	Large basins, long simulations, teaching examples	Fast, stable, robust	Oversmoothed terrain; weak small-channel representation


## Climate Fles
-   gridded product formatting
    -   ascii format
    -   netcdf format
-   local station data formatting
-   base station

## Definition Files
-   Format - required (ID) and optional parameters
-   Base Files/Default values
-   Parameter file library
-   some parameters may need expanded information, i.e. phenolog flag: static, dynamic, drought

## Generating the Worldfile and Flowtable

### The Template File

-   Format and Initialization
    -   World objects
    -   State variables
    -   Default and base station ID's
-   Required and Optional state variables
-   Editing the template, setting initial conditions

### Aspatial rules file for Multi-scale routing

-   Format
-   Set up for single rule
-   Set up for multiple rules - requires map

### RHESSysPreprocessing R package

-   About
-   Prerequisites: spatial data, template, asp rules file
-   R markdown workflow/script - functions
-   Run the function to generate Worldfile/Flowtable
